goal: Refresh CLAUDE.md to match v2.0 substrate layout: src/slop replaces src/cli/slop, new packages (tree/language/structure/lexicon/linter/cli/config/schemas/app), Violation→Slop, slop.app:main entry, per-substrate rule registries, rule-add procedure updated for (view, rc, sc) signature

summary.title: Refresh CLAUDE.md for v2.0 substrate layout
summary.what: Rewrote CLAUDE.md (95 insertions, 30 deletions) to match the post-cutover repo: src/slop replaces src/cli/slop as the package root; sections updated for repo-layout (tree/language/structure/lexicon/linter/cli/config/schemas + legacy kernel survivors), rule-add procedure (substrate selection, (view, rc, sc) signature, per-substrate registry append), and design decisions (per-substrate aggregated at slop.linter; cmd.py convention; Result.json()/pretty() split). Added a v2.0 cutover-notes section listing retired surfaces (slop.engine, slop.models, slop.output, slop.rules, Violation, RuleDefinition.run_v2, SLOP_ENGINE env var) so future agent sessions don't reach for them.
summary.why: The previous CLAUDE.md described the v1.x monolithic layout under src/cli/slop with central rules registry and slop.cli:main entry — all of which retired in commits 9917743 + 9538481. Any future session bootstrapping from the stale doc would get a wrong mental model and reach for removed surfaces.
summary.user_goal: Refresh CLAUDE.md to match v2.0 substrate layout: src/slop replaces src/cli/slop, new packages (tree/language/structure/lexicon/linter/cli/config/schemas/app), Violation→Slop, slop.app:main entry, per-substrate rule registries, rule-add procedure updated for (view, rc, sc) signature

decisions.chose: Document legacy kernels (_ast, _fs, _text, _compose, _structural, _lexical, _util) as 'still load-bearing on dev' rather than dropping them from the layout description.
decisions.rationale: 29 of 31 rules still call into these via legacy_v2_shim. A doc that doesn't mention them would mislead readers into thinking they were already retired.

decisions.chose: Added an explicit 'v2.0 cutover notes' section listing every retired Python symbol with its replacement.
decisions.rationale: Pre-existing agent memory and any session that cached the v1.x surfaces would otherwise reach for slop.engine / slop.rules / Violation. One terse list is faster to scan than rediscovering each removal via import errors.

decisions.chose: Rule-add procedure expanded from 5 steps to 6, with substrate-selection as step 1.
decisions.rationale: v2.0 splits rules across three packages (structure/lexicon/linter) by responsibility. New-rule authors need to make that decision first; without it the procedure is ambiguous about which __init__.py to append to.

review_notes: Layout claims spot-checked: app.py delegates to slop.cli.main; DEFAULT_RULE_CONFIGS and generate_default_config exist in slop/config/loader.py; rule counts confirmed (STRUCTURAL_RULES=20, LEXICAL_RULES=9, CROSS_CUTTING_RULES=2 = 31 total).
v2.0 cutover notes section explicitly mirrors the api_changes from intent int_0e914600's seal, ensuring a future agent reading either source gets the same retired-surface list.

architectural_claims: CLAUDE.md is the authoritative layout reference for future agent sessions; the in-repo doc supersedes any cached or memory-stored layout.
